free_text: Elastic scattering of neutrons from N15
The
experimental method used to obtain the angular
distributions.
For neutron energies below 150 keV, the
Li-7(p,n)Be-7 reaction served as a source of
neutrons. A lithium nitride target 39 keV thick at
threshold was used for these measurements. At the
higher energies, neutrons were produced by bombarding
a 30 keV thick Zr-T target with protons.
A proportional counter filled to a pressure
of 25 mm of Hg with 96% enriched N-15 was irradiated
with a collimated beam of neutrons.
Translated from SCISRS.
Converted to reaction formalism.
BP: Updated to new date formats, lower
case. Corrections according last EXFOR rules and
Dict. Updated entry, corrected title, added doi,
REL-REF, INC-SOURCE, DETECTOR, SAMPLE. Digitized data.
The measured angular distributions were
extrapolated smoothly to zero degrees and integrated
over angle. These integrated angular distributions
were then normalized to unit charge collected on the
target and divided by the forward yield of the
appropriate source reaction. The resulting relative
total cross sections obtained with the two
neutron-source reactions were normalized to each
other at a neutron energy of 750 keV, where the cross
section varies slowly with energy. Absolute total
cross sections were obtained by normalization of
these relative cross sections to the data of Sikkema
in the vicinity of 1920 keV neutron energy.
A relative uncertainty estimated to be
about eight percent is introduced into these
measurements by the uncertainty in the extrapolation
of the angular distributions to zero degrees.
The uncertainties in the relative yields of
the neutron-source reactions average about five
percent.
Sikkema has stated that the
systematic errors in his absolute cross sections may
amount to 10-15%.
Also shown in Fig.2, page 52.
Reaction corrected.
